reasons: Lolita can deny Bruno's front-facing ranged attack pressure with Guardian's Reflection.
Bruno depends on repeated enhanced basic attacks, so shield timing can reduce his main damage window.

proof.summary: Bruno wants to land repeated enhanced ranged attacks, while Lolita can reflect ranged basic attacks and projectile pressure from the shield direction.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces Bruno before his enhanced attack connects.
Allies stay behind Lolita during Bruno's damage window.
failureCases: Bruno attacks from a side angle.
Lolita uses shield too early and Bruno waits it out.

reasons: Natalia can pressure Bruno from conceal before he freely cycles enhanced basic attacks.
Bruno is much easier to punish when he is separated from peel or vision.

proof.summary: Bruno wants visible and controlled DPS space, while Natalia can threaten from conceal and force him to play closer to protection.
worksBestWhen: Natalia controls bushes around Bruno's rotation path.
Bruno is away from hard crowd-control peel.
failureCases: Bruno stays grouped with tank or support protection.
Natalia is revealed before reaching Bruno.

reasons: Saber can lock Bruno with targeted burst before Bruno has time to kite.
This is especially punishing when Bruno's dash or defensive battle spell is unavailable.

proof.summary: Bruno wants to kite and keep attacking, while Saber can create a short targeted airborne burst window that denies Bruno's DPS uptime.
worksBestWhen: Saber attacks from fog or a flank angle.
Bruno has no immediate peel or defensive cooldown.
failureCases: Bruno survives the burst with support protection.
Saber dives into a grouped enemy team and gets traded.

reasons: Belerick punishes repeated basic attacks and can force Bruno to hit a durable frontline instead of a carry.
Taunt and frontline pressure can reduce Bruno's freedom to choose safe attack targets.

proof.summary: Bruno wants to repeatedly attack priority targets, while Belerick can absorb pressure, punish basic attacks, and disrupt Bruno's target choice.
worksBestWhen: Belerick stands between Bruno and the backline.
Belerick has enough durability to survive Bruno's damage window.
failureCases: Bruno kites Belerick and attacks from max range.
Belerick cannot reach Bruno or force him to hit the frontline.

reasons: Franco can punish Bruno's positioning with hook or suppression before Bruno outputs sustained damage.
Bruno is vulnerable when caught during rotation or before he has space to kite.

proof.summary: Bruno wants safe spacing for repeated attacks, while Franco can remove that spacing by hooking or suppressing him when he steps too far forward.
worksBestWhen: Franco hides vision before Bruno rotates.
The team follows up immediately after hook or suppression.
failureCases: Bruno stays behind minions or frontline protection.
